rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion To Quash Subpoenas. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem Judge has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Of Petition For Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER DANIEL BOREEN's Motion Of Petition For Administrative Mandamus. Continued to September 8, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 21, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Amended Arbitration Award Dated February 9, 2014
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 2, RESPONDENT VINCENT ISCH's Petition To Vacate Amended Arbitration Award Dated February 9, 2014. Denied as moot in light of the ruling on the Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RUDOLF V ISCH VS. VINCENT A ISCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Petition To Confirm Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER RUDOLF ISCH's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Granted. (CCP sec. 1285.) Per CCP sec. 1288, a motion to vacate or correct an award must be filed within 100 days after service of the award. (See also Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1211, holding that the 100 day period is jurisdictional and the court has no discretion to extend the mandatory deadline.) The same 100-day limitation applies when vacation or correction of the award is sought by response. (CCP sec. 1288.2; De Mello v. Souza (1973) 36 Cal.App.3d 79, 83.) It is undisputed that the Amended Arbitration Award was served on February 10, 2014. (Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award, par. 9(b); Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award, par. 9(b).) Respondent's Answer and Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award were not filed until July 16, 2014, beyond the 100 day period, and are therefore time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ROSE WARD VS. JULIA DUKHOVNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MARY WARD's CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The claim is denied. Mr. Duchovny claims that the funds are separate property. See CCP 703.020(b)(2). The court will execute the provided judicial council form. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ROSE WARD VS. JULIA DUKHOVNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg Of Third Party Claim
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MARY WARD's Hearing Of Third Party Claim. The court orders the funds returned to Mr. Duchovny. A hearing to resolve the issue was not calendared in a timely fashion pursuant to CCP 720.310(a). Even if the court had the jurisdiction to entertain the third party claim, the court would rule in favor of Mr. Duchovny. Mr. Duchovny's evidence indicates that the couple was separated at the time of the bike accident and plaintiff cannot levy against his separate property. See Family Code Sec. 910(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CATALINO CARINO et al VS. ALIREZA KATOUZIAN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT SPANISH HILLS SURGERY CENTER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Triable issues of material fact exist as to whether Dr. Katouzian was the ostensible agent of Defendant. For example, the parties dispute whether Dr. Katouzian ever disclosed to Plaintiff that Dr. Katouzian was not an agent or employee of Defendant (Plaintiff's Additional UMF #4), and whether Plaintiff only saw Dr. Katouzian at Spanish Hills Surgery Center (Plaintiff's Additional UMF #3). In addition, Defendant's attempt to deny that Plaintiff was never informed by anyone that Dr. Katouzian was not Defendant's employee and that Plaintiff at all times believed him to be Defendant's agent or employee, none of the evidence proffered in support contradicts those assertions. (See Plaintiff's Additional UMF #6). Defendant points to nothing in its evidence indicating that Plaintiff received or was aware of either the referral form or any of Dr. Katouzian's Consultation Reports. Furthermore, the Consent Form contains Defendant's logo and address as a header, and is silent as to the relationship between Defendant and Dr. Katouzian excepting the notice that Dr. Katouzian has a financial interest in the Center. There is no reason to infer that this disclosure explicitly or implicitly alerted Plaintiff that Dr. Katouzian was not the employee or agent of Defendant. Additionally, Defendant's general reference to over 200 pages of medical records-without further explanation or citation-fails to support its denial. Finally, Defendant's contentions and evidence bearing on whether Dr. Katouzian was actually an agent of Defendant have no relevance in determining whether Defendant can be liable on a theory of ostensible agency. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR LLC, VS. JENNIFER M. PEYTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR LLC's, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHAD FOLKERS VS. SAFEWAY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF CHAD FOLKERS Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Records. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem Judge has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FOUZIA HDOUBANE VS. GOLDEN GATE HOTEL INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 21, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS GOLDEN GATE HOTEL INC., JOHN KENASTON, RENATE KENASTON and GABRIELE KENASTON's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Continued to September 12, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 21, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the [moving/opposition/reply] papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
